title: The unwritten rule
desc: A neighbour points out a faux pas
setting: It's Sunday morning and you've started the washing machine. Your neighbour knocks, friendly but pointed.

steps.speaker: Nachberin
steps.es: Grüezi. Säged Sie, wäsched Sie grad? Am Sunntig?
steps.en: Hello. Tell me, are you doing laundry? On a Sunday?

options.es: Oh, isch das es Problem? Das han ich nöd gwüsst, Entschuldigung.
options.en: Oh, is that a problem? I didn't know that, sorry.
options.feedback: Open, humble, ready to learn — disarms it instantly.

options.es: Ja, han ich öppis falsch gmacht?
options.en: Yes, did I do something wrong?
options.feedback: Fine, but a hint of defensiveness — receive the correction gently.
Technically maybe, but this attitude sours years of neighbourly peace.

steps.speaker: Nachberin
steps.es: Am Sunntig isch Rueh, gäll. Mer wäscht da nöd, das stört.
steps.en: Sunday is a quiet day, you see. You don't do laundry then, it disturbs.

options.es: Verstande, das mach ich ab jetz anders. Merci fürs Säge.
options.en: Understood, I'll do it differently from now on. Thanks for telling me.
options.feedback: Gracious and practical — she'll remember you as the good kind of newcomer.

options.es: Aber d'Maschine isch doch nöd so laut.
options.en: But the machine isn't that loud.
options.feedback: Arguing the point misses it — the rule is about the day, not the decibels.
It isn't written anywhere — that's exactly the kind of rule you just accept.

steps.speaker: Nachberin
steps.es: Schön, dass Sie's verstönd. Die meischte Nöie wüssed das nöd.
steps.en: Nice that you understand. Most newcomers don't know it.

options.es: Ich lärne gärn dezue. Gits no anderi söttigi Reglä?
options.en: I'm glad to learn. Are there other rules like that?
options.feedback: Turning a telling-off into curiosity — brilliant. She'll happily help you now.
A touch dismissive after she was patient — a warmer note costs nothing.

options.es: Es git aber scho viel z'merke.
options.en: There's an awful lot to keep track of, though.
options.feedback: True and human, but framing it positively keeps her on your side.

steps.speaker: Nachberin
steps.es: Gärn erklär ich Ihne mal alls bi me Kafi.
steps.en: I'd happily explain it all to you over a coffee sometime.

options.es: Das wär würkli nett, sehr gärn. Merci!
options.en: That would be really kind, I'd love to. Thanks!
options.feedback: A scolding just became a friendship. That's the whole art of it.
She's extending real warmth — meet it with a clearer yes.

options.en: I don't want to bother you.
options.feedback: Polite, but she offered — accepting honours the gesture.
